review_summary: The transcript clearly supports two questions on chaining and visual programming, so no extra clip is required.

question_bank.question: What does chaining mean in the context of multimodal foundation models?
choices: Combining multiple specialized models or tools so the system can solve tasks no single model handles well alone
Training one larger model by merging all datasets into a single epoch
Restricting a model to answer only questions about one image at a time
Replacing all multimodal models with a single object detector
question_bank.explanation: Different models have different strengths. Chaining uses those strengths together, such as combining detection, language, or arithmetic modules to answer a harder question.

question_bank.question: What is the core idea behind VisProg-style visual programming?
choices: Generate a program that calls specialized vision tools and combines their outputs to answer the question
Train a new multimodal model from scratch for each query
Use only CLIP retrieval without any tool execution
Replace visual inputs with hand-written symbolic rules only
question_bank.explanation: Instead of forcing one end-to-end model to do everything internally, visual programming decomposes the task into tool calls and operations that can be executed step by step.
